channel.title: bothSidesHeadlinesNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from bothSidesHeadlinesNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Heart's Physical Forces May Help Suppress Cancer Cell Development, Study Suggests
item.description: Research conducted in mice indicates that the mechanical pressure generated by a beating heart may act as a natural barrier against the proliferation of cancer cells.

item.title: Researchers Identify Previously Unknown Mechanism for How the Body Regulates Sugar
item.description: Scientists at WEHI have described a newly discovered biological process governing sugar storage in the body, a finding they say challenges established biochemical principles.

item.title: U.S. Child Mortality Rates Higher Than Peers in Other Wealthy Nations, Study Finds
item.description: A study from Children's Hospital researchers found that children in the United States face higher death rates across all age groups compared with those in comparable high-income countries.

item.title: Alcohol Linked to More Cancer Cases in Australia Than Previously Estimated
item.description: New research from the University of Sydney suggests alcohol consumption contributes to a higher share of cancer diagnoses in Australia than earlier studies had indicated.

item.title: SCORE2-LAC Model Aims to Improve Cardiovascular Risk Prediction Across Latin America
item.description: A new preliminary framework for estimating cardiovascular risk in Latin America and the Caribbean was presented at the ESC Preventive Cardiology conference.
